entries.gameplayFunction: Base worker population for gathering, hauling, and visible settlement life.
entries.visualRole: Worker baseline and population readability anchor.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Simple tools, clear carrying posture, low-threat read.
entries.materialNotes: Coarse cloth, rope, light leather, simple timber tools.
entries.colorNotes: House color on small cloth accents only.
entries.scaleNotes: Human-scale baseline for all civilian comparisons.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Keep grounded and readable; avoid peasant caricature.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs shared rig, carry variants, and house tint hooks.

entries.gameplayFunction: Visible construction and repair labor family.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Hammer, mallet, rope, timber frame cues.
entries.materialNotes: Aprons, gloves, heavier belts, work wraps.
entries.scaleNotes: Same body scale as villager with more gear bulk.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must read as active construction crew, not combat unit.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs build-loop animation set and scaffold interaction poses.

entries.gameplayFunction: Emergency levy infantry and first military read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Mixed hand weapons, simple shields, uneven armor.
entries.colorNotes: Shield paint and tabard edge carry House cues.
entries.scaleNotes: Five-person squad silhouette with shared health presentation.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Weak but believable. Not ragged bandits.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs squad member visual degradation states.

entries.gameplayFunction: Early professional infantry and reconnaissance-capable line unit.
entries.silhouetteNotes: One-handed blades, cleaner shield discipline, faster posture.
entries.materialNotes: Leather, partial iron, fitted military cloth.
entries.colorNotes: House cloth panels and shield emblems.
entries.scaleNotes: Five-person squad with tighter formation than militia.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Keep cleaner than militia without becoming knightly.
entries.unityImportNotes: Shared infantry rig with House-specific shield meshes.

entries.gameplayFunction: Defensive line holder and anti-cavalry infantry family.
entries.visualRole: Reach weapon line and defensive silhouette.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Long spear read must survive zoom and formation overlap.
entries.materialNotes: Wood shafts, shields, layered cloth and partial mail.
entries.colorNotes: House paint on shield and pennon only.
entries.scaleNotes: Longest infantry reach family in early lane.
entries.generationPromptNotes: No fantasy-long pikes yet; keep stage-appropriate.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs reach-safe animation spacing and formation offsets.

entries.visualRole: Readable ranged lane at gameplay height.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Bow arc, quiver mass, light armor profile.
entries.materialNotes: Wood bows, cloth, leather bracers, limited iron.
entries.colorNotes: House cloth accents and quiver tags.
entries.scaleNotes: Lighter silhouette than melee line units.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Keep lines clean enough to read behind infantry.
entries.unityImportNotes: Requires projectile-ready draw and release timings.

entries.gameplayFunction: Light skirmish and ambush family from population roots.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Short bow, javelin, or hunting gear read depending variant.
entries.colorNotes: Low saturation; House cues remain small.
entries.scaleNotes: Lighter than bowmen and closer to civilian base.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Avoid turning into fantasy ranger archetype.
entries.unityImportNotes: Useful as future biome-aware or faith-aware branch.

entries.gameplayFunction: Shock cavalry and stage escalation landmark.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Horse armor, lance or sword profile, high-speed wedge read.
entries.materialNotes: Iron, leather tack, cloth caparison kept restrained.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Keep grounded and practical; no tournament fantasy.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs mounted rig strategy and charge-state animation pass.

entries.gameplayFunction: Light cavalry, scouting, harassment, and convoy disruption.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Lean horse, light rider kit, scouting pennon optional.
entries.materialNotes: Leather, light iron, rope, saddle bags, survival gear.
entries.colorNotes: House cloth accents only; motion should carry read more than color.
entries.scaleNotes: Smaller and lighter than knight family.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Should read raider and scout, not noble cavalry.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs readable gallop, raid, and intercept poses.

entries.gameplayFunction: Repair, breach support, and siege-line service.
entries.visualRole: Technical support unit that is clearly non-frontline.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Tools, rope, hooks, measuring staff, compact sidearm only.
entries.materialNotes: Canvas, leather aprons, gloves, iron tools.
entries.colorNotes: Minimal House tags on sleeve or shoulder cloth.
entries.scaleNotes: Human scale with tool bulk read.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must not be mistaken for worker or infantry.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs repair, tending, and brace interaction animations.

entries.visualRole: Logistics silhouette and convoy readability anchor.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Wagon frame, draft animal, cargo mass, driver silhouette.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, canvas, rope, casks, sacks, iron banding.
entries.colorNotes: House cloth tags and cargo cover stripes.
entries.scaleNotes: Larger than cavalry footprint but lighter than siege engine.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Readable from far zoom as logistics, not civilian cart.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs escort binding points and cargo-state swaps.

entries.gameplayFunction: Visible battlefield command presence and House authority.
entries.visualRole: Command-unit identification and morale anchor read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Clear leader massing, bodyguard separation, command banner control.
entries.materialNotes: Best-in-family armor and cloth quality without fantasy hero excess.
entries.colorNotes: Highest allowed House accent concentration short of UI portrait use.
entries.scaleNotes: Command presence through composition, not giant body scale.
entries.generationPromptNotes: More refined than line infantry but still RTS readable.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs portrait alignment and command-marker hooks.

entries.gameplayFunction: Visible civic authority for keep, city, and border governance lanes.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Escort, clerk, or steward grouping depending role seat.
entries.materialNotes: Fine cloth, administrative tools, lighter armor for escorts only.
entries.colorNotes: House trim and office marks rather than battlefield saturation.
entries.scaleNotes: Human-scale grouped silhouette for keep or settlement scenes.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must read as governance, not court spectacle.
entries.unityImportNotes: Primary use is keep view, settlement scenes, and portraits.

entries.gameplayFunction: House-defining elite military lane reserved for asymmetry and late authority.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Stronger House-specific shape language than generic roster.
entries.materialNotes: Best House materials, clearer heraldic discipline, tighter equipment build.
entries.colorNotes: House palette strongest here but still controlled.
entries.scaleNotes: Reads as elite through posture and gear quality, not giant scale.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Use House identity pack as primary source.
entries.unityImportNotes: Likely one family per House once canon locks mature.

entries.gameplayFunction: Live Ironmark unique melee lane tied to blood-production burden.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Heavy axe head, dense torso armor, disciplined brutal economy of motion.
entries.materialNotes: Charcoal iron, worn leather, ember-cloth accents.
entries.colorNotes: Ironmark ember and iron values only.
entries.scaleNotes: Heavier than generic swordsman, not superhuman.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must communicate cost, weight, and industrial discipline, not berserker fantasy.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs unique icon, weapon silhouette, and squad degradation variant.

entries.gameplayFunction: Live Hartvale unique defensive-support melee lane.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Polearm or defensive warding profile, layered agrarian guard silhouette.
entries.materialNotes: Warm metal, leather, harvest cloth, protected rural gear.
entries.colorNotes: Hartvale orange-gold and deep red with only subtle green support where approved.
entries.scaleNotes: Heavier and calmer than generic infantry, still RTS clean.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Should read cultivated protector, not druid fantasy.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs unique icon and settlement-support readability checks.

entries.gameplayFunction: Covenant-driven unit lanes from level-3 onward.
entries.visualRole: Distinct faith overlay without erasing House identity.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Use covenant-specific markers, not generic fantasy magic.
entries.materialNotes: Faith props and garment treatment layered on House base kit.
entries.colorNotes: Faith colors are overlays and secondary to House command color discipline.
entries.scaleNotes: Comparable to elite or specialist units depending family.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Never let faith treatment fully replace House lineage read.
entries.unityImportNotes: Should likely use modular overlays and shared base rigs.

entries.gameplayFunction: Reserved lane for future dynasty-specific civic specialists once canon locks them.
entries.visualRole: Non-military House asymmetry in labor, governance, or support expression.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Driven by House identity pack until gameplay family locks.
entries.materialNotes: Use House civic materials, not elite military kit.
entries.colorNotes: Controlled House palette, office marks, role tools.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Do not invent gameplay; reserve visual lane only.
entries.unityImportNotes: Hold in staging until future canon names and functions settle.
